title: A Lesson About Feelings and Work
article_text: Last week, my mom told me that it is vital to understand our own feelings. She had always been fascinated by how external events can affect our mood. Whenever I feel sad, she reminds me that depression is not a sign of weakness. I once felt guilty because I had not spared enough time for my family. My mom explained that hidden worries often underlie our anger and stress. She said that the satisfaction of helping others can sweep away negative thoughts. I have incorporated her advice into my daily routine, and I no longer stare at problems anymore. She also taught me that every obligation we take on should be evaluated carefully. Sir, I perceive that this lesson has changed my perception of work and life. Now I understand the distinction between a busy life and a meaningful one, and I often enjoy a quiet drink at a pub to relax.

sentences.en: Last week, my mom told me that it is vital to understand our own feelings.
sentences.zh: 上周，我妈妈告诉我，理解自己的感受至关重要。

sentences.en: She had always been fascinated by how external events can affect our mood.
sentences.zh: 她一直着迷于外部事件如何影响我们的情绪。

sentences.en: Whenever I feel sad, she reminds me that depression is not a sign of weakness.
sentences.zh: 每当我感到悲伤时，她提醒我抑郁不是软弱的表现。

sentences.en: I once felt guilty because I had not spared enough time for my family.
sentences.zh: 我曾感到内疚，因为我没有为家人腾出足够的时间。

words.eng_def: feeling that you have done something wrong
to give time or money for a purpose

sentences.en: My mom explained that hidden worries often underlie our anger and stress.
sentences.zh: 我妈妈解释说，隐藏的担忧常常是愤怒和压力的根源。

words.eng_def: to be the cause of something
a strong feeling against someone or something

sentences.en: She said that the satisfaction of helping others can sweep away negative thoughts.
sentences.zh: 她说帮助他人的满足感可以扫除消极的想法。

words.eng_def: a good feeling when you get what you want

sentences.en: I have incorporated her advice into my daily routine, and I no longer stare at problems anymore.
sentences.zh: 我已经把她的建议融入我的日常生活，我不再盯着问题看了。

words.eng_def: to include something as part of something
a usual or fixed way of doing things
to look at something for a long time

sentences.en: She also taught me that every obligation we take on should be evaluated carefully.
sentences.zh: 她还教我，我们承担的每一项义务都应该仔细评估。

words.eng_def: to judge the value or quality of something

sentences.en: Sir, I perceive that this lesson has changed my perception of work and life.
sentences.zh: 先生，我察觉到这堂课改变了我对工作和生活的看法。

words.eng_def: a polite way to address a man
the way you think about something

sentences.en: Now I understand the distinction between a busy life and a meaningful one, and I often enjoy a quiet drink at a pub to relax.
sentences.zh: 现在我明白了忙碌生活和有意义生活之间的区别，我经常在酒吧安静地喝一杯来放松。

words.eng_def: a clear difference between two things
a place where people go to drink and meet friends

vocabulary.eng_def: very important or necessary
vocabulary.example: It is vital to drink water every day.

vocabulary.eng_def: to interest someone very much
vocabulary.example: Space fascinates many children.

vocabulary.eng_def: coming from outside
vocabulary.example: External noise can make it hard to sleep.

vocabulary.eng_def: to give time or money for a purpose
vocabulary.example: Can you spare a few minutes to help me?

vocabulary.eng_def: every time that something happens
vocabulary.example: Whenever I call, she answers.

vocabulary.eng_def: a feeling of great sadness
vocabulary.example: Depression can affect anyone.

vocabulary.eng_def: feeling that you have done something wrong
vocabulary.example: He felt guilty about lying.

vocabulary.eng_def: to be the cause of something
vocabulary.example: Fear often underlies anger.

vocabulary.eng_def: mother
vocabulary.example: My mom makes the best cookies.

vocabulary.eng_def: a clear difference between two things
vocabulary.example: There is a distinction between right and wrong.

vocabulary.eng_def: a good feeling when you get what you want
vocabulary.example: Finishing a job gives me satisfaction.

vocabulary.eng_def: to include something as part of something
vocabulary.example: We should incorporate new ideas.

vocabulary.eng_def: to make a liquid flow from a container
vocabulary.example: She poured water into the glass.

vocabulary.eng_def: to remove something completely
vocabulary.example: The wind will sweep the leaves away.

vocabulary.eng_def: something you must do
vocabulary.example: Paying taxes is an obligation.

vocabulary.eng_def: a polite way to address a man
vocabulary.example: Thank you, sir.

vocabulary.eng_def: to judge the value or quality of something
vocabulary.example: Teachers evaluate students' work.

vocabulary.eng_def: a strong feeling against someone or something
vocabulary.example: She tried to control her anger.

vocabulary.eng_def: a place where people go to drink and meet friends
vocabulary.example: We met at the pub after work.

vocabulary.eng_def: the way you think about something
vocabulary.example: His perception of the problem changed.

quiz.question_cn: 哪个词的意思是“非常重要的”？
哪个词的意思是“悲伤的强烈感觉”？
quiz.question_en: Which word means 'a strong feeling of sadness'?

quiz.question_cn: 哪个词的意思是“构成...的基础”？
quiz.question_en: Which word means 'to be the cause of something'?

quiz.question_cn: 哪个词的意思是“你如何看待某件事”？
quiz.question_en: Which word means 'the way you think about something'?

quiz.question_cn: 哪个词的意思是“你必须做的事”？
quiz.question_en: Which word means 'something you must do'?
